name: nails
summary: NAILS — NixOS Anti-forensics Isolation & Layering System

documentation.readme: NAILS — NixOS Anti-forensics Isolation & Layering System
usage_sections: a visible decoy NixOS installation that should remain mundane and plausible
a hidden real environment stored on a mounted backend you control
NixOS-specific workflow using declarative rebuilds and profile switching
Storage-agnostic backend model as long as the backend is already mounted and writable
Overlay-based isolation so writes go to hidden storage rather than the decoy base system

arguments.name: --allowed-leaf
arguments.description: Allowed live leaf. Repeat for multiple leaves.

arguments.name: --expected-requested-target
arguments.description: Required requestedTargets value in the resolved plan.

arguments.name: --github-output
arguments.description: Optional GITHUB_OUTPUT file path.

arguments.name: --github-step-summary
arguments.description: Optional GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY file path.

arguments.name: --iterations
arguments.description: Requested iteration count to validate and pass to the planner.

arguments.name: --max-iterations
arguments.description: Maximum allowed iteration count.

arguments.name: --min-iterations
arguments.description: Minimum allowed iteration count.

arguments.name: --plan-json
arguments.description: Use an explicit plan JSON payload instead of invoking the planner script.

arguments.name: --plan-script
arguments.description: Planner script used when --plan-json is not provided.

arguments.name: --summary-title
arguments.description: Markdown heading used when writing a GitHub step summary.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Requested target to validate.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/WitteShadovv/nails /mnt/hidden/src/nails. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo cryptsetup open --type tcrypt --veracrypt --tcrypt-hidden \`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo mount /dev/mapper/veracrypt-hidden /mnt/hidden`; `install -m0755 /path/to/downloaded/nails /mnt/hidden/nails`. NAILS — NixOS Anti-forensics Isolation & Layering System.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: NAILS — NixOS Anti-forensics Isolation & Layering System.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: NAILS — NixOS Anti-forensics Isolation & Layering System.

Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with simsong/bulk_extractor (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
